patch_type: Universal model trace inspector and calibratable trust model registry over V60.2 Evidence-to-Score Impact Preview
release_chain_status: V60.3 is the current implemented build. It loads after V60.2 in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js. It adds the Universal Model Trace Inspector and calibratable Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Model registry while preserving V60.2 evidence-to-score impact previews, no-new-top-level-tabs, and prototype boundary language. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. We have high confidence in the architecture, medium confidence in the initial scoring factors, and lower confidence in default weights until calibrated against test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
uploaded_requirements_source: CyberShield V60.3 Trust Model Registry and Scoring Model Requirements Addendum uploaded by Dr. Justice; controlling product doctrine for V60.3 model registry and trace inspector work.
v60_3_changes: labels models as Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models
explicitly avoids claiming statistically validated trust models
adds Universal Model Trace Inspector UI
adds calibratable metadata including model version, factor weights, thresholds, override notes, calibration history, test scenario results, limitations, and human review notes
adds core calibratable model definitions for Evidence Trust, Confidence, Reliance Risk, Runtime Admissibility, Proof Pack Defensibility, and Human Verification Maturity

adds placeholder roadmap models for Internet Trust, Vendor Trust, AI Agent Trust, Purpose Alignment, Identity Trust, Data Trust, Control Trust, Policy Trust, Trust Debt, Agentic Action Risk, Prompt Injection Exposure, Tool Use Risk, and Organizational Trust Maturity

universal_model_trace_inspector.boundary: Static advisory prototype only. The registry defines expert-derived V1 calibratable models. It does not perform live scoring, live evidence retrieval, statistical validation, certification, legal determination, workflow automation, ticketing, notification, backend persistence, or enforcement.

calibration_requirements_for_90_plus_con: 20 to 50 test scenarios across internet trust, vendor trust, AI agent action, policy/control trust, and executive decisions
edge cases where trust and confidence diverge
expert review from cybersecurity, legal, audit, and AI governance perspectives
red-team attempts to manipulate the scores
score calibration after observing false positives and false negatives

trust_evidence_workbench.known_issue: Full Manual Evidence Workbench appears in Runtime and should be corrected so Runtime receives a compact evidence requirements panel while Evidence keeps the full workbench.

recommended_next_build.label: V60.3.1 Runtime Evidence Panel Correction
goals: remove full Manual Evidence Workbench from Runtime
keep full Manual Evidence Workbench in Evidence
keep proof-oriented evidence trace in Proof Pack
add compact Runtime Evidence Requirements panel to Runtime
preserve no-new-top-level-tabs and prototype boundary language

implemented_release_train.v56: Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer
implemented_release_train.v59.5: Internet Trust QA and Copy Guardrail Pass

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, statistical validation, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Models are expert-derived V1 and not statistically validated
Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative
V60.3 does not perform live scoring
V60.3 does not retrieve live evidence
V60.3 does not persist data to a backend
V60.3 does not create tickets or send notifications
V60.3 does not perform workflow automation
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
